questions.question: Which institution releases the ‘World Giving Index 2021’?
questions.explanation: The World Giving Index (WGI) for the year 2021 has been released by the Charities Aid Foundation (CAF). The report states that COVID 19 and associated lockdowns have changed the giving behaviour of countries across the globe. India stands at 14th most generous countries in the world. Indonesia has been ranked at the 1st spot.

questions.question: Which state announced Rs 1 lakh relief to each BPL family, that lost earning member due to COVID?
questions.explanation: Karnataka Chief Minister B S Yediyurappa announced Rs 1 lakh relief to each family under Below Poverty Line (BPL) which has lost an earning or adult member due to COVID-19. This decision will benefit about 25,000 to 30,000 such families incurring an expenditure of Rs 250 crore to Rs 300 crore to the state.

questions.question: Where is the Pine Island Glacier located?
questions.explanation: The Pine Island Glacier is one of the largest ice streams in Antarctica and is the fastest melting ice shelves in the region. As per a recent study by the Washington University and British Antarctic Survey, the ice shelves of Pine Island Glacier are reported to rapidly break down. It is expected to collapse faster than previously expected timeline. Its complete melting could result in 0.5 meters rise in global sea levels.

questions.question: Which Union Ministry has launched ‘Smart enforcement application’ for Trucks, to reduce Logistics costs?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways

questions.explanation: The logistics division of the Commerce Department of Union Ministry of Commerce & Industry has launched an IT-based smart enforcement app, which aims to implement of rules and regulations related to road-based violations by trucks in India. This move is expected to reduce logistics costs and transport time in India by decreasing the number of physical checks of trucks.

questions.question: Which Indian state recently decided to adopt the Andhra Pradesh government’s flagship scheme, “Nadu Nedu”?
questions.explanation: The Telangana government recently decided to adopt the Andhra Pradesh government’s flagship scheme, Nadu Nedu. The Telangana government has recently sought a “no objection certificate” from the Andhra Pradesh government over the Mana Badi Nadu Nedu. Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh YS Jagan Mohan Reddy launched the ‘Nadu-Nedu’ programme which seeks to transform government schools into vibrant and competitive institutions.

questions.question: Paraceratherium, that was recently un news, is an extinct genus of which animal?
questions.explanation: Paraceratherium is an extinct genus of hornless rhinoceros. It is one of the largest terrestrial mammals. The exact size of Paraceratherium is unknown because of the incompleteness of the fossils. It lived in habitats ranging from arid deserts with a few scattered trees to subtropical forests.

questions.question: Queshuachaca Bridge, that was recently in news, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: Queshuachaca Bridge is in Quehue District, Canas Province, Peru. The dimension of the bridge is 33 m. long and 1.20 m. wide, the material used is only the braided straw, obtained from the high Puna. It requires an annual maintenance because the straw used for its construction begins to decompose.

questions.question: ‘Varuna and Assi’ rivers, which were seen in the news recently, flow through which Indian state?
questions.explanation: The National Green Tribunal has set up a committee for rejuvenation of rivers Varuna and Assi in Varanasi, both of which joins the river Ganga. This move has been made by the tribunal after taking note of the reported discharge of untreated sewage in these rivers and unauthorised constructions on the banks of these rivers.

questions.question: Which water body is also called the Gulf of Barbera?
questions.explanation: The Indian Navy, for the first time is participating in a joint exercise with the European Union Naval Force (EUNAVFOR), which is set to take place in the Gulf of Aden, also known as the Gulf of Barbera. This exercise would involve some of the most advanced air defence, anti-submarine exercises, cross deck helicopter operations, tactical manoeuvres etc.

questions.question: Which Chief Minister is representing India in the Progressive International summit for Covid-19 vaccine internationalism?
questions.explanation: A four-day summit by Progressive International for Covid-19 vaccine internationalism has recently begun. It aims to provide vaccines for all people across the world. The summit will bring together governments, politicians, healthcare workers and vaccine manufacturers from over 20 countries. From India, Kerala Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan with former health minister- KK Shailaja, representatives of ASHA workers and vaccine maker Virchow Laboratories are participating in the summit.
